title: tiquilia plicata
intro: Tiquilia plicata, also known as desert holly or plicated tiquilia, is a plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. It is native to the southwestern regions of the United States and Mexico, and is often used for its medicinal and herbal properties. Tiquilia plicata has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce the risk of infection. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions and wounds.
